threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think it's normal to call it the 'Underground' instead of just 'the Tube.' Everyone here knows it’s a whole different vibe.
Last week on the 45, I saw a guy trying to sell origami cranes. Said they bring good luck—everyone just stared, but he was so earnest.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When you see the same guy selling falafel on the corner, but one day he’s drumming along to a saxophonist while the sun sets. That’s home.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: You ever notice the dogs getting a free ride on the U-Bahn? They just hop on like they own the place. No fuss, just Berlin life.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s a local when they casually walk past a BART closure and still make it to brunch at Tartine on time, no panic.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Just saw a guy in a tuxedo giving a live serenade to his date while we waited for the Muni. Only in SF, right?

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists always miss that Gràcia isn’t just about Gaudí. The real magic is in the little plazas, where locals chat over morning coffee, not selfies.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They still ask for 'taps' instead of 'pintxos' when ordering at the bars. You can spot them a mile away.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it 'the Tube' instead of just ‘the underground’—it’s a dead giveaway that you’re not a local yet.
Last night on the 35, the guy next to me pulled out a full-size piano keyboard. I swear he played Bach all the way to Clapham. Only in Brixton!

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: I found a random pop-up flea market in the courtyard of an old school, filled with vintage records and homemade jams last Sunday. Total Neukölln vibe.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin can you see people partying on a tram during a streik, making the best of public transport chaos. It’s like a mobile club vibe!

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a true local by how they instinctively step around the dog poop on Valencia without losing stride.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a Muni bus late last night—two musicians set up on the back steps, playing a duet while we creep through the fog. Just a Tuesday in SF.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think they can find proper catalan pa amb tomàquet in every bar, but only the locals know the secretos of the family-run places.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a newcomer by their confused expression when the street performers start a heated debate over a game of chess on the sidewalk.
